rcomplex 0.1.0
  • Reference
  • Articles
    • Comparative co-expression analysis with rcomplex

Sweep density thresholds for robustness assessment

Source: R/comparison.R
density_sweep.Rd

Reruns the full pairwise comparison pipeline at multiple threshold multipliers to assess how sensitive conservation results are to the choice of density cutoff.

density_sweep(networks, ...)

# Default S3 method
density_sweep(
  networks,
  orthologs,
  multipliers = seq(0.95, 1.05, by = 0.01),
  method = c("permutation", "analytical"),
  alternative = c("greater", "less"),
  alpha = 0.05,
  n_cores = 1L,
  use_torch = FALSE,
  min_exceedances = 50L,
  max_permutations = 10000L,
  ...
)

Arguments

networks

Named list of compute_network outputs.

orthologs

Data frame with columns Species1, Species2, hog.

multipliers

Numeric vector of threshold multipliers (default seq(0.95, 1.05, by = 0.01)).

method

Comparison method passed to find_coexpressologs: "permutation" (default) or "analytical".

alternative

"greater" (conservation, default) or "less" (divergence).

alpha

Significance threshold (default 0.05).

n_cores

Number of threads (default 1).

use_torch

Logical; GPU acceleration for permutation method (default FALSE).

min_exceedances

Besag-Clifford parameter for permutation method (default 50).

max_permutations

Maximum permutations (default 10000).

Value

A data frame with columns multiplier, eff_density, n_significant, and edges (list-column of edge data frames).

Details

For each multiplier, every network's threshold is scaled by threshold * multiplier, the effective density is recorded, and find_coexpressologs is called on the modified networks.

See also

find_coexpressologs, clique_persistence

Examples

if (FALSE) { # \dontrun{
sweep <- density_sweep(networks, orthologs,
                        method = "permutation", use_torch = TRUE)
} # }
